programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard travels to England to investigate Britain's Roswell, also known as the Woodbridge-Bentwaters UFO incident, considered by many to be one of the best documented UFO cases on record

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several independent researchers about the efforts of international bankers to control the course of history and enslave humanity through their monopoly of the creation of money and the manipulation of the money supply.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Could vampires be real? A well-known U.S. paranormal investigator and folklorist in New York City recounts the legend and evolution of the vampire throughout history and from culture to culture.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the pre-eminent investigators of the alleged crash of a UFO near Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Ruthless Killers
programme.desc: Ruthless Killers: They kill for all manner of reasons. Their victims: men, women, and even children. What motivates them, and how can they be stopped? Investigators reach their limits hunting them down.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Bank Robbers
programme.desc: Bank robbers. They are clever, planning their crimes for years. And if necessary, they even put human lives at risk. For investigators, each heist is different. But all robbers have one thing in common: they steal millions.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: University students Holly Dunn and Chris Maier are ambushed along railway tracks in August 1997. By 1999, a string of similar attacks leads police to Angel Maturino Reséndiz, who travels by freight train between murders across three states.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1983, Graeme Cunningham vanishes in Salt Lake City. Detective Don Bell discovers Arthur Bishop, who confesses to killing Graeme and four other boys. Bishop is found guilty in 1984 and executed in 1988.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Murder by the Mob depicts the battle for justice by Peppino Impastato's family after his 1978 death was misclassified. His mother and brother reopened the case, leading to mafia boss Gaetano Badalamenti's 2002 conviction for the murder.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The future of recycling; how GPS works and how it is changing the world; using science to crack a magic trick.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Living Planet
programme.desc: With continuous observation of arth from orbit scientists have built a data set over time that reveals much about how the Earth breaths and life sustains itself on this living Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Cosmic Collisions

programme.title: The New Frontier: Gravity
programme.desc: LISA pathfinder, has led the way for a space based gravity wave detector, what are gravity waves and why are they so hard to detect?

programme.desc: Winter hits Elwood City and Arthur sees dollar signs! His snow-shoveling business is booming--that is, until Buster decides to get in on the action. And, Brain can be a know-it-all, and sometimes he really bugs people.

programme.title: Arthur: George Scraps His Sculpture/Arthur's Big Meltdown

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Tv-Free Week/Night Fright
programme.desc: The kids and parents of Ellwood City have all pledged not to watch TV for one whole week. Can they last? Then, big, brave Binky Barnes would be toast if people ever discovered he can't sleep without a night light.

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Barnes, Art Expert/Arthur's Lucky Pencil
programme.desc: It's a nightmare--Arthur and Buster have to work with Binky. On an art project! I mean, what does a bully like Binky know about art? Then, Arthur discovers the secret to success--a pencil that brings good luck to whoever possesses it.

programme.title: Arthur: Big Horns George/Bleep
programme.desc: George is inspired to write and perform his own blues song when he's introduced to the music of Koko Taylor. And, D.W. overhears a mysterious word she doesn’t know the meaning of. Can D.W. risk the consequences of blurting out <"BLEEP!">?

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Bombing of Ploesti
programme.desc: American bombers target a Romanian oil refinery in a daring 1943 mission.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Monte Cassino (September 3, 1943)
programme.desc: As the allies make their way into Italy, they encounter tremendous resistance at German defensive positions along the Gustav line. One of these locations is an ancient monastery called Monte Cassino.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: The Gilbert Islands (November 1943)
programme.desc: Bloody fighting erupts on a group of tiny Pacific islands in November 1943.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of Anzio
programme.desc: Allied armies encounter German resistance in the Italian fishing town of Anzio.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Kwajalein
programme.desc: Examining the battle at the Kwajelein Atoll in the South Pacific during 1944. Included: operations by the Seabees.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle in Palau (Summer 1944)
programme.desc: As the United States prepared for the invasion of the Philippines, scheduled for late 1944, the tiny Palau Islands had to be taken first.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: D-Day (June 6,1944)
programme.desc: D-Day, the 6th of June--one of the most pivotal moments in world history. In a massive armada of thousands of ships, 3,000,000 allied troops land at Three Beaches in Normandy, France.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Retaking France
programme.desc: Six weeks after the D-Day invasion of the Beaches of Normandy, the allied forces are pushing the Germans back through the French countryside. Town by town is won back as Panzer divisions retreat back to the Meuse River.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Operation Dragoon (August 1944)
programme.desc: The Allies break into France and push the Germans back to their own border.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Liberation of the Paris
programme.desc: Both political and military expediency were factors in the return of the French capital to its people.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The Battle of Leyte Gulf tells the story of the largest sea conflict in history involving Pearl Harbor warships and the largest battleships in the world, and how the US Navy, despite kamikaze attacks and a Japanese decoy fleet, were victorious.

programme.title: History Of Weapons: Deadly Inventions
programme.desc: Wars become even bloodier as new technologies continue to evolve. An international team of experts tests the deadliest weapons upgrades in the history of warfare.

programme.title: Guns That Changed The Game: Rise Of The Machines
programme.desc: Rise of the Machines: While the single shot rifle remained a mainstay until WWI, machine guns had been in development since the final days of the American Civil War.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft: Boeing B-29: Superfortress
programme.desc: The story of the prolonged development and the initially troubled operational history of the Boeing B-29 Superfortress. Forever known as the airframe that dropped the first atomic weapons on Japan, the B-29 lived on well beyond its war years.

programme.title: Hitler's Engineers
programme.desc: Wernher von Braun was vital to NASA's Apollo 11 mission to put a man on the moon, but this 'American hero' had his Nazi past kept under wraps.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Explore the design flaws and human errors that condemned great ships to a tragic end. In 1628 the warship Vasa sank on her maiden voyage. Together with Vasa curator Fred Hocker, solve the mystery of why this great ship sank.

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys

programme.title: Guardians Of The Wild
programme.desc: Watch three orphaned elephants learn the skills needed to survive in the bush.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Mystery of Disembodied Feet

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Abbotsford Killer

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime

programme.title: The New Detectives: In The Camera's Eye

programme.title: The New Detectives: Blind Trust
programme.desc: A woman's body is found and investigators have little time and few clues to lead them to a killer who could strike again. Perpetrators try to conceal their crimes, yet savvy investigators can take the most obscure data and recreate a murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Elements Of Murder
programme.desc: There are several things that can suggest murder; jealousy, anger, poison. When everyday substances reveal hidden clues that break a homicide case, those substances become the elements of murder.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Deadly Chemistry

programme.title: The New Detectives: Critical Evidence

programme.title: Laguna Beach: The Best Part of Breaking Up....

programme.title: Laguna Beach: The Last Dance

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The legend of the werewolf throughout history and in various cultures is explored. The Conspiracy Show travels to England and rural Wisconsin to investigate claims of actual werewolf sightings.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Manchurian Candidates
programme.desc: Several prominent researchers in the field provide a short history of Mind Control experimentation beginning with the Nazis through to the CIA's infamous MK Ultra program.

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Mysterious Stone Monuments of Markawasi Peru
programme.desc: Travel high into the Peruvian Andes to explore the strange, wondrous and unexplained mysteries of stone formations that may have been carved by thousands of years of erosion -- or the hands of a long-dead civilization

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Afterlife Investigations
programme.desc: Narrated by Britain's leading investigative Journalist - Donal MacIntyre - this award winning film presents four never before seen scientific investigations into life after death.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MEGAFLOODS
programme.desc: Water covers two thirds of the earth’s surface, and we are utterly dependent on it, but when this vital ingredient of life unleashes its fury on the planet it becomes the ultimate bringer of death.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Venezuela Lightning
programme.desc: Gas from Catatumbo swamps mixed with cold air from the Andes create a perpetual lightning storm on South America's largest lake. From here George canoes 84 km to world's highest waterfall, swims with piranhas and holds a huge Anaconda.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Volcanic Wedding
programme.desc: Most people's idea of a dream wedding might include a horse drawn carriage and an elaborate ceremony. Not George. Join him as he and his bride to be tie the knot in an unusual and certainly exotic way.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Hyabusa
programme.desc: The Japanese Asteroid sample return mission has reached its target and is surveying the space rock ready to dip down and take a sample.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Fresh Eyes
programme.desc: Two new space telescopes are in orbit and completing their shakedown; Gaia and TESS they too will be joined by the James Webb Telescope.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Martian Attractions
programme.desc: The Latest in Martian exploration from orbit and on the ground, new sources of water and planetary dust storms are a few of the stories unfolding on to the Red Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Heavy Lift
programme.desc: New heavy lift rockets are taking to the sky. Falcon Heavy, SLS, Arianne 6 and Vega C rockets are primed to begin the next phase of space expansion.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Proving Einstein
programme.desc: Some of the proof of Einstein’s theories had long been elusive, beyond the capabilities of technology, until now.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Where Are All the Aliens?
programme.desc: We’re looking hard, but the numbers aren’t stacking up, is life on other planets that hard to find? Even signals from an advanced race elude us.

programme.title: STAR LIGHT, STAR BRIGHT
programme.desc: The two current science missions to our star; the sun have made great leaps forward in the science of how stars work, how they generate solar winds and how they affect their orbiting planets

programme.title: MOON SHINE
programme.desc: The International drive to return to the Moon is gathering pace, this multi- nation effort is readying to return, stay and utilise the Lunar assets.

programme.title: RISING TIDES
programme.desc: The next generation of Earth observation satellites are now in place maintaining the continuity of long-term climate data, in particular the planets rising temperatures and related ocean levels.

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN
programme.desc: Venus is a planet closest to Earth in distance and size, it once may have been as hospitable to life as Earth is now. Then something changed and Venus became shrouded in hot dense toxic clouds.

programme.desc: The Forth Rail Bridge has grown to symbolise Scotland.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: New Mexico has the largest Hispanic population per capita in the US. No better way to see the mash-up of cultures that make up this uniquely American State, than by what the people here are eating

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Arthur: Muffy's Art Attack/Tales from the Crib
programme.desc: When Muffy sees a kinetic sculptor’s machines, she brags that she can make even better ones. Then, Vicita is upset at having to trade in her cherished crib for a big-girl bed, so D.W. shares her story of that same rite of passage.

programme.title: Arthur: The Wheel Deal/The Buster Report
programme.desc: After an injury, Brain has to spend some time using a wheelchair. Will he be able to compete in a basketball tournament for charity? The kids are assigned to write a report on someone they admire, and George picks….Buster!

programme.desc: Muffy's proud that her dad is the new soccer coach--until he exhausts the team with demanding drills. The try-outs for the Young Person's Orchestra are coming to Lakewood Elementary and everyone agrees that Binky is a shoe-in.

programme.title: Arthur: Hic or Treat/Mr. Alwaysright
programme.desc: D.W. gets a case of the hiccups that just won't go away. Will she have them for the rest of her life and set the world record? It seems that Brain is always right about everything and it's really starting to get on Buster's nerves.

programme.title: Arthur: Sue Ellen's Lost Diary/Arthur's Knee

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster

programme.desc: The pilot of Swissair Flight 111 smells smoke in the cockpit. Minutes later fire and complete electrical failure cause the MD11 to crash into the Atlantic killing all 229 passengers and crew on board.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Aeroperu Flight 603 bound from Lima, Peru to Santiago, Chile shows how a simple error and a lack of flight crew training can bring a giant, complex airliner to a deadly end.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Foolish

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the Big MACC
programme.desc: At an army testing lab where a super laser is being demonstrated, the crowd is surprised by the arrival of MACC, a robot from the year 2399 that talks like a cowboy from watching old western movies on inter-dimensional trips.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Ninja Sword of Nowhere
programme.desc: Krang detects the existence of an alien metal that could help him conquer the Earth. It happens to be on a Ninja Sword the Turtles have purchased for Splinter as a National Sensei Appreciation Day gift.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): 20,000 Leaks Under the City

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Take Me to Your Leader

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Four Musketurtles

programme.desc: The city's new state-of-the-art garbage disposal system's main computer DUMP 1000 is programmed by Shredder to seize the Turtles but starts scooping up every turtle in sight.

programme.desc: With the aid of the Holographic Synthesizer, Shredder creates an exact likeness of Michelangelo and programs it to invade the Turtles' lair and destroy them.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Turtle Snatchers

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings draw inspiration from lessons they find in a martial arts book and start breaking every wooden object they can get their hands on into two. When Grizzy tries to stop them, they go on to the next lesson and get him in a judo hold.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dreams that he's a valiant knight who has delivered his beloved. As he is about to take her in his arms, he is woken by the Lemmings who have chosen this day to blast music.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzly imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.desc: Dean Stockwell guest stars as Gunnar Malstrom, one of the leaders of Pendulum -- a secret terrorist organization -- who has a plan involving a major attack on the government that the Impossible Missions Force is assigned to uncover.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Western
programme.desc: Ed Nelson guest stars as Van Cleve, a brilliant thief who is convinced by the IMF that he has the power of precognition as part of its plan to recover the more than five million dollars worth of art treasures that he has stolen.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Catch My Drift?
programme.desc: The guys land a custom drift car and a luxury antiques unit that could have a one-of-a-kind pilot pistol from WWI. While trying to track down the provenance of the gun, Ton's lead foot gets them into trouble at their car sale.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Whiskey Business
programme.desc: An auction in Allen's old stomping grounds triggers memories of being a "lone wolf." The guys find a set of RC race boats and when they buy a man-cave unit full of old whiskey, a night of partying leads to some tough sales.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Reel Money
programme.desc: 1970's-era Fighting Chair used for big-game fishing; military MILES "laser tag" training system.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Chicago Grand Slam
programme.desc: 1984 Yamaha SS440 Snowmobile that introduced the revolutionary shock absorber suspension; rare autographed 1920s baseball glove; mint Hank Aaron signed baseball; authentic 1950s Robby the Robot collector toy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hall of Fame Game
programme.desc: Allen heads to an auction with Elle, while Ton and Big Sis revamp the shop to help bring in more customers. Allen unearths a Ouija board and a set of antique basketballs, and goes toe-to-toe with an NBA Hall of Famer.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Dallas Mavericks
programme.desc: Military-grade Tomahawk cruise missile jet engine; century-old handcrafted wild west cowboy saddle; 1960s high roller's gold money.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Party Gras
programme.desc: On a trip to New Orleans, Ton and Allen look to mix business with pleasure. They wage war with some locals at a tough auction, win a Mardi Gras unit that contains a confetti blaster and a modern calliope, then hit the town.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Tough as Nails
programme.desc: CJ seeks to disrupt Allen's search for serenity at a storage auction before the guy's head to a live auction to buy and sell. They uncover an unusual Marilyn Monroe statue, an industrial slip n' slide, and a bed of nails.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: To Pawn or Not To Pawn
programme.desc: On the brink of a major decision, Ton and Allen face-off with Carolyn at an auction in Covina. They win a "special effects unit" that contains big machines for crazy stunt work. The guys decide the fate of the shop.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen's Got Balls
programme.desc: After returning to LA, Ton & Allen head deep into hostile auction hunter territory and butt heads with a familiar old foe! They discover a driving range golf ball picker vehicle and one of the most accurate rifles ever made!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Louisiana Purchase
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Ton and Allen encounter some salty locals at a shipping crate auction, and navigate choppy waters at a boat auction. After spending a lot of cash, they find a tactical blast shield and a bass fishing boat.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: George Worgan

programme.desc: Sharks are the ocean’s ultimate predator. At the top of the food chain, many species of sharks are capable of killing quickly and efficiently anything they attack.

programme.title: Wild Assassins: Beaks and Talons
programme.desc: Welcome to the world of wild assassins, where masters of the sky sweep from above with their fierce beaks and razor sharp talons.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators: Italian Bears
programme.desc: No one would have bet on the result of the introduction of seven Slovenian bears, two males and five females, in the woods of Adamello, in Trentino.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Oh Brother, Where Art Thou?
programme.desc: With a long record of violence and abuse and weighing in at over 200lbs, Dog's target for the day does not sound like a gentle giant. When the trail grows cold Dog turns up the heat on the fugitive's brother and sister.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Cap and Gown
programme.desc: Friend of the family, Kili, is joining the team to help track down a runner on the Hawaiian island of Maui. This is not the only thing on their schedule, because after the hunt, Kili has to hurry off to his college graduation ceremony!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: And Baby Makes Three
programme.desc: Leland has to attend a Lamaze class before he can join the hunt--another Chapman is on the way! But when Dog starts to go after this accused jewelry thief without his fastest son, he learns that this fugitive puts the "R" in runner.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Tender Trap
programme.desc: When Dog discovers today's prey is a notorious ladies man, he knows just how to lure him in. The trap includes a suggestive phone call, a motel rendezvous, and a crew of bounty hunters waiting in the next room ready to break up the party!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Greed is Good
programme.desc: Today's fugitive is accused of assaulting a police officer, so Dog and the team are not taking any chances. Dog has to play on his target's greed and deploy some modern technology to set a trap for this runner.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Tears for Fears
programme.desc: Baby Lyssa wakes up in a jail cell, but ignores the ensuing media frenzy in order to befriend a woman waging a personal battle against drug addiction.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Stumped on a way to celebrate Willie's birthday, Korie enlists the guys' help and Jase takes the reigns in planning a trip to the lake where Phil and Kay used to live. Meanwhile, the women realize a raccoon is inside the lake house.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Glory Is the Reward of Mallard
programme.desc: When Willie takes a business trip to Scotland to expand his duck call business, he brings the entire family along so they can reconnect with their Scottish roots. Willie organizes a bus tour to trace his family heritage.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Good Night and Good Duck
programme.desc: A special holiday episode of Duck Dynasty shows when the Robertson family took over the Thanksgiving special of a local TV show "Good Morning Arklamiss." Each family member presents a special holiday segment, with Willie and Jase serving as anchormen.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack in the Saddle
programme.desc: When Willie becomes the temporary owner of a thoroughbred, he and the guys take it to a local track and enter it into a race. Meanwhile, when Kay decides she wants to buy a new birdbath for her yard, she recruits Phil, Martin and Godwin.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: A Home for the Holidays
programme.desc: The Robertsons help a long time employee whose Mom has fallen on hard times by buying her a brand new house for Christmas. Jep, Jase, Martin and Godwin race to finish a deck in time for the big reveal, only to get sidetracked by a hammering competition.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Cannonball Runs
programme.desc: Jase enters an outhouse race to help promote Willie's Duck Diner, but when the Robertsons show up for the race, they face an unexpected rival - Phillip McMillan. Meanwhile, Willie attempts to create the world's greatest bologna sandwich.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Mo Math, Mo Problems
programme.desc: Willie helps Sadie with her homework by enlisting the guys to create a real-life version of a math problem, but when Jase and Willie get sidetracked helping Sadie, they rekindle an old argument of whether Jase can outrun a truck.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Coop! There It Is!
programme.desc: When Jep asks Willie and the guys to help move his old chicken coop into his new house, which is under renovation, Jessica takes issue with its appearance, leading the guys to yet another project - renovating the chicken coop.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Friday Afternoon Lights
programme.desc: When Willie becomes an assistant coach on Lil' Will's struggling football team, his authority is tested when the kids challenge the adults to a scrimmage. Meanwhile, Kay brings Jep and Si to an aquarobics class at a local health club.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Master and Duck Commander
programme.desc: When Jase and the guys transform an old pontoon boat into a floating duck blind, they are faced with the impending question of whether or not it will actually float. Meanwhile, Kay's dog Bobo misbehaves.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: All That Glitters
programme.desc: Jonathan and Mark pose as priests in order to re-open an abandoned church as a shelter when a major fire breaks out in a neighborhood.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Wally
programme.desc: Wally is a hobo and street puppeteer whose caring and goodness have made him an angel without him knowing it.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: A Song of Songs
programme.desc: A struggling pianist tries to start life again after a heart attack.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: A Night to Remember
programme.desc: High school seniors hope they get the dates they want for prom.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: A Mother and a Daughter
programme.desc: An estranged daughter is writing a vengeful expose about her mother, a much-beloved movie star.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Doomsday at Yellowstone: Scientists seek to explain the unexplainable while investigating blue fire near Yellowstone, turkeys circling a dead cat and a Gettysberg ghost.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Mayan Treasure: An explorer at a Mayan sacrificial site is attacked by an underwater fog, thousands of fish jump to their deaths, and a UFO is spotted in LA skies.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Omen of Blood River: A river of blood washes through a city in Bangladesh, an ominous noise scares a Canadian town and a giant hole appears in the clouds above California.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: America's Loch Ness Monster: A Loch Ness Monster-esque creature is recorded in America, experts analyse evidence of a giant scaly egg, and birds are vaporized by a death ray.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Monster Spider: A massive spider's web takes over a park, and experts investigate if this monster's trap could capture a human. A tree spurts out thick red blood.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Beast That Ate Jaws: A shark is eaten by a mysterious predator, and experts wonder if a prehistoric monster is to blame. A sinister sight is filmed near Russia's Area 51.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Gown
programme.desc: Alexis learns Galen is alive in Moldavia; Jeff plans to move to California; Adam and Claudia are married; Krystle pays a surprise visit to Sammy Jo and spots her double, Rita.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Titans (Part 1) and (Part 2)

programme.title: Dynasty: The Decision

programme.title: Dynasty: The Proposal

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Solana & Elijah
programme.desc: Solana is in a relationship with Danny but can't stop thinking about her online love Elijah. His photos were used in so many profiles that she has to know is this the real Elijah?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Miranda & Cameryn
programme.desc: After years of online chats Miranda discovered that Cameryn was not the man he claimed to be. She confronted him only to be given another suspicious profile. Is he someone else entirely?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jeff & Megan
programme.desc: After a string of bad luck with women, Jeff believes he has finally found his dream girl Megan. However, Megan is starting to sound off Catfish alarms.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Bianca & Brogan
programme.desc: When Brogan disappears from the Internet, then returns online after a year without question, Bianca wants to know what happened in the missing year.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Miracle & Javonni
programme.desc: Smart, single mum Miracle fell for Javonni, a dreamy-eyed Instagrammer. With Max on hiatus, Nev teams up with TV personality Charlamagne to take Miracle on a hunt for the man of her dreams. (S4, ep1)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Courtney & Isaak
programme.desc: Courtney broke up with Isaak, who she met online. He's doing everything to get her back. Beauty queen Cassidy Wolf joins Nev to get Isaak to leave Courtney alone for good. (S4, ep2)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Harold & Armani
programme.desc: Harold found his match with a beautiful fellow jock named Armani. Although they live in the same area, they've never met. Nev is joined by Angel Haze to help Harold get his perfect girl. (S4, ep3)

programme.title: Teen Mom: A Little Help

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Afterlife Investigations
programme.desc: Narrated by Britain's leading investigative Journalist - Donal MacIntyre - this award winning film presents four never before seen scientific investigations into life after death.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES
programme.desc: Scientists, engineers, military, and intelligence agencies push boundaries with unlimited budgets and secrecy. This episode unveils known (or suspected) top-secret projects.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: THE MYSTERY AND CULTS OF MUMMIES
programme.desc: Unlock the mystery of mummification from ancient Egypt to present. Explore its fascination and quest for Queen Nefertiti's mummy. Where did 70 million ancient mummies disappear? Delve into Latin American mummy legends.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MEDICAL MYSTERIES
programme.desc: Medical history is rife with jaw-dropping oddities, miraculous recoveries, and unsolved mysteries. Beyond shock value, these cases showcase human resilience and the vast unknowns in understanding the body and mind.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2004, Marc Dutroux was sentenced to life for kidnapping, raping, and murdering young girls in Belgium. His 1996 arrest and delayed trial exposed horrific crimes and official failings, sparking public outrage.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Volcanic Wedding
programme.desc: Most people's idea of a dream wedding might include a horse drawn carriage and an elaborate ceremony. Not George. Join him as he and his bride to be tie the knot in an unusual and certainly exotic way.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Timbuktu: Birthplace of Hurricanes
programme.desc: Dust storms can spin off from Africa into the Atlantic and morph into tropical hurricanes that threaten the Caribbean and USA. While searching for Timbuktu's dust storms, George finds adventure in the landscape and culture of West Africa.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hawaii: Surf & Snow
programme.desc: In Hawaii George explores the wild side: diving with Galapagos sharks, ascending to snow-covered Mauna Kea, dental cleaning by Peppermint Cleaner Shrimp, extreme surfing at Banzai Pipeline, getting his boots lit on fire by Kilauea volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Boiling Lake
programme.desc: Tucked away, high in the mountains of the Caribbean island of Dominica lies a marvel of nature  a lake whose water literally boils and steams like a witch's cauldron.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Waterspouts
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, George uses a motorboat, a van and small plane to get up close to waterspouts - the nautical cousins of tornadoes. He also meets some of the 25 million alligators that live in the Sunshine State.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Blizzards
programme.desc: Winter weather can be even more dangerous than thunderstorms and where else to explore the big chill than at the summit of Mount Washington which has the reputation as being the place with the "Worst weather in the world".

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN
programme.desc: Venus is a planet closest to Earth in distance and size, it once may have been as hospitable to life as Earth is now. Then something changed and Venus became shrouded in hot dense toxic clouds.

programme.title: The New Frontier: INCREASING ORBIT
programme.desc: The ISS is the workhorse of the international spacefaring community, but its days are numbered. R

programme.title: The New Frontier: GIANTS IN THE DARK
programme.desc: Asteroids, the debris from the early solar system hold tantalizing clues to planetary formation. The spacecraft Psyche readies to study a unique metallic asteroid.

programme.title: The New Frontier: OCEANS IN THE SKY
programme.desc: Juice and the Europa Clipper are two missions to our Solar System’s largest planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: WEBB OF LIFE
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope is now operational. Launched aboard an Arianne 5 the 10-billion-dollar spacecraft has deployed successfully and reached its orbit around the Sun.

programme.title: The New Frontier: PEERING INTO THE ABYSS
programme.desc: Imaging a black hole was a breakthrough in astronomy, now with the new satellite IXPE high energy telescope we will be looking at some of the most dramatic, and most violent objects in the universe: Colliding Black holes and Neutron Stars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: THE NEXT BIG LEAPS
programme.desc: Projects in the works. SunRISE, SPHEREx, NEOS and others, their mission to expand our knowledge of Earth, the surrounding space, Dark Matter, and the earliest galaxies. N

programme.desc: Cold War & The Start of the Space Race - Cold War rivalry and the acceleration of space exploration. Servicing Hubble - Preparations for Hubble's final servicing mission. Chasing a Comet - Launch and the difficult path to rendezvous.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Mercury Begins - The start of NASA & America's first manned space program. Mars Express - The European Space Agency's Mars Satellite. The Soyuz Launch System - The gradual evolution of Russia's Soyuz launcher.
